agent_output:
{
  "domain_analysis": {
    "business_context": "Digital wallet platform enabling peer-to-peer and outbound money transfers with tiered identity verification, regulatory compliance (KYC/AML/sanctions screening), and account security controls. The product operates in a regulated financial services space where compliance failures carry legal and financial penalties.",
    "bounded_context": "Wallet Transfers & Account Security",
    "ubiquitous_language": [
      { "term": "KYC (Know Your Customer)", "definition": "Identity verification process that determines a user's verification tier and unlocks sending/withdrawal capabilities." },
      { "term": "Verified Account", "definition": "An account that has completed full KYC. Permitted to send, receive, and withdraw funds up to the daily transfer limit of $5,000." },
      { "term": "Partially-Verified Account", "definition": "An account that has completed some but not all KYC steps. Can send funds up to a $500 daily transfer limit but cannot withdraw." },
      { "term": "Unverified Account", "definition": "An account that has not begun or completed any KYC. Can receive funds only — cannot send or withdraw." },
      { "term": "Daily Transfer Limit", "definition": "The maximum cumulative outbound transfer amount permitted within a rolling or calendar day, determined by verification tier." },
      { "term": "Settlement (T+1)", "definition": "The point at which a transaction becomes final — one business day after initiation. Prior to settlement, a transaction is pending; after settlement, it is irreversible." },
      { "term": "Sanctioned Entity", "definition": "A person, organization, or jurisdiction appearing on applicable sanctions lists (e.g., OFAC SDN). Transfers to sanctioned entities must be blocked before execution." },
      { "term": "Account Lock", "definition": "A security state triggered by 5 consecutive failed login attempts. A locked account cannot perform any operations until a support agent manually unlocks it." },
      { "term": "High-Value Transaction Notification", "definition": "An alert sent to the account holder for any transaction exceeding $1,000 in value." }
    ]
  },

  "business_rules": [
    {
      "id": "BR-1",
      "rule": "An unverified account MUST NOT initiate a send or withdrawal transaction. Only receiving is permitted.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Allowing unverified users to move money out of the system violates KYC/AML regulations. This is a legal compliance constraint — if violated, the system is operating illegally.",
      "testable_assertion": "Any attempt to send or withdraw from an account with verification_status = 'unverified' must be rejected with an appropriate error."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-2",
      "rule": "Verified accounts have a daily outbound transfer limit of $5,000. Partially-verified accounts have a daily outbound transfer limit of $500.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "The specific dollar thresholds are business policy decisions that could be adjusted by product or compliance leadership without corrupting system state.",
      "testable_assertion": "The sum of all outbound transfers for an account within a calendar day must not exceed the limit for its verification tier ($5,000 verified, $500 partially-verified). Any transfer that would exceed the remaining daily allowance must be rejected."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-3",
      "rule": "A transaction CANNOT be reversed once it has reached settled status (T+1).",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Settlement finality is a core financial integrity constraint. Reversing a settled transaction would corrupt the ledger and create double-spend scenarios. This is a data consistency invariant.",
      "testable_assertion": "Any reversal request against a transaction with status = 'settled' must be rejected. Only transactions with status = 'pending' (pre-settlement) may be reversed or cancelled."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-4",
      "rule": "Transfers to sanctioned entities MUST be blocked before execution. The transfer must never be initiated.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "This is a legal/regulatory constraint (OFAC, EU sanctions). Executing a transfer to a sanctioned entity is a federal violation. If this invariant is broken, the company faces criminal liability.",
      "testable_assertion": "When a transfer recipient matches a sanctioned entity list, the transaction must be blocked, never debited from the sender, and flagged for compliance review."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-5",
      "rule": "After 5 consecutive failed login attempts, the account MUST be locked. Only a support agent can unlock it.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "This is an account security integrity constraint. If a locked account can be unlocked by any mechanism other than a support agent, the security model is broken — brute-force attacks become viable.",
      "testable_assertion": "After 5 consecutive failed logins: (1) the account status must transition to 'locked', (2) all subsequent login attempts — even with correct credentials — must be rejected, (3) only an authenticated support agent action can transition the account back to 'active'."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-6",
      "rule": "A notification must be sent to the account holder for any transaction exceeding $1,000.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "The $1,000 threshold and the notification preference are business policies. A VP could change the threshold to $500 or $2,000 without corrupting system state. Failure to notify is a UX issue, not a data integrity violation.",
      "testable_assertion": "When a transaction with amount > $1,000 is created (sent or received), a notification event must be emitted targeting the account holder. Transactions of exactly $1,000 or less do not trigger this notification."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-7",
      "rule": "An unverified account CAN receive money without restriction.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Allowing unverified accounts to receive is a product policy to reduce onboarding friction. This could be changed to require verification for receiving as well without breaking system integrity.",
      "testable_assertion": "An inbound transfer targeting an account with verification_status = 'unverified' must succeed (assuming sender is authorized and no other rules are violated)."
    }
  ],

  "acceptance_criteria": [
    {
      "id": "AC-1",
      "given": "A user with an unverified account and a positive wallet balance",
      "when": "The user attempts to send money to another user",
      "then": "The transaction is rejected with an error indicating KYC verification is required, and no funds are debited"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-2",
      "given": "A user with an unverified account",
      "when": "Another verified user sends them $200",
      "then": "The transfer succeeds and the unverified account's balance increases by $200"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-3",
      "given": "A verified user who has already sent $4,800 today",
      "when": "They attempt to send an additional $300",
      "then": "The transaction is rejected because it would exceed the $5,000 daily limit, and no funds are debited"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-4",
      "given": "A partially-verified user who has sent $0 today",
      "when": "They attempt to send $501",
      "then": "The transaction is rejected because it exceeds the $500 daily limit"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-5",
      "given": "A transaction that was initiated yesterday and has now reached T+1 settlement",
      "when": "The sender requests a reversal",
      "then": "The reversal is rejected with a message indicating the transaction has been settled and is irreversible"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-6",
      "given": "A transaction that was initiated today and is still in pending status (pre-settlement)",
      "when": "The sender requests a cancellation or reversal",
      "then": "The transaction is cancelled, funds are returned to the sender's available balance"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-7",
      "given": "A verified user initiating a transfer to a recipient",
      "when": "The recipient matches an entry on the sanctions list",
      "then": "The transfer is blocked before any funds are debited, the attempt is logged for compliance review, and the user is informed the transfer cannot be completed"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-8",
      "given": "A user with an active account who has entered incorrect credentials 4 times consecutively",
      "when": "They fail a 5th consecutive login attempt",
      "then": "The account is immediately locked, and subsequent login attempts (even with correct credentials) are rejected with a message to contact support"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-9",
      "given": "A locked account",
      "when": "An authenticated support agent issues an unlock command",
      "then": "The account is unlocked, the failed login counter is reset, and the user can log in normally"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-10",
      "given": "A user with a locked account",
      "when": "The user attempts to unlock the account themselves via password reset or any self-service flow",
      "then": "The account remains locked — only a support agent can unlock it"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-11",
      "given": "A verified user sending $1,500 to another user",
      "when": "The transaction is successfully created",
      "then": "A notification is sent to the account holder informing them of the $1,500 transaction"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-12",
      "given": "A verified user sending exactly $1,000 to another user",
      "when": "The transaction is successfully created",
      "then": "No high-value transaction notification is sent (the threshold is 'over $1,000', not 'at or over')"
    }
  ],

  "edge_cases": [
    { "scenario": "User completes KYC verification mid-day after already receiving funds while unverified", "expected_behavior": "The user's tier upgrades immediately. They can now send up to their new daily limit. Previously received funds are available for sending. The daily limit window applies from the time of the first outbound transfer that day, not from the verification moment." },
    { "scenario": "A transfer would bring the sender exactly to the $5,000 daily limit (e.g., $4,500 sent + $500 new transfer)", "expected_behavior": "The transfer succeeds — the limit is $5,000, and the total equals exactly $5,000. Only amounts that would exceed the limit are rejected." },
    { "scenario": "Recipient is added to the sanctions list after a transfer is initiated but before T+1 settlement", "expected_behavior": "This is ambiguous in the spec. Recommended behavior: the pending transaction should be flagged and frozen for compliance review before settlement occurs. Sanctions screening should occur at initiation AND at settlement." },
    { "scenario": "User has 4 failed logins, then one successful login, then 5 more failed logins", "expected_behavior": "The successful login resets the consecutive failure counter to 0. The account locks after 5 NEW consecutive failures, not cumulative total failures." },
    { "scenario": "A locked account receives an inbound transfer", "expected_behavior": "The spec does not address this. Recommended: inbound transfers to locked accounts should still be accepted — the lock is a security control on authentication, not a freeze on the wallet itself. However, compliance may require otherwise." },
    { "scenario": "A partially-verified user attempts to withdraw funds", "expected_behavior": "The spec says unverified accounts cannot withdraw but is silent on partially-verified withdrawal. This needs clarification — the most conservative reading is that only fully verified accounts can withdraw." },
    { "scenario": "Notification delivery fails for a >$1,000 transaction", "expected_behavior": "The transaction should NOT be rolled back due to notification failure. Notifications are a side effect, not a precondition. Failed notifications should be retried and logged." },
    { "scenario": "A single transaction of exactly $5,000 from a verified account with no prior transfers today", "expected_behavior": "The transaction succeeds — it is within the daily limit." },
    { "scenario": "A user initiates a transfer to themselves (same wallet)", "expected_behavior": "Not addressed in the spec. Self-transfers should likely be disallowed as they serve no business purpose and could be used for limit manipulation or money laundering layering." }
  ],

  "business_impact": {
    "financial": "Incorrect daily limit enforcement could expose the company to losses beyond risk tolerance. Sanctions violations carry penalties from $50K to $20M+ per violation under OFAC. Settlement reversal bugs could create ledger imbalances and direct financial loss.",
    "operational": "Account locking mechanics directly affect support team volume. If the lock threshold is too aggressive, support is flooded. If too permissive, fraud risk increases. Sanctions screening latency affects transfer throughput and user experience.",
    "user": "KYC gating creates onboarding friction — allowing receive-only for unverified accounts is a smart UX compromise. Account locking with support-only unlock creates frustration but is necessary for security. The $1,000 notification threshold builds user trust and transparency.",
    "risk": "HIGH — this system operates in a regulated financial domain. BR-1 (KYC gate), BR-3 (settlement finality), and BR-4 (sanctions blocking) carry legal/regulatory risk if violated. BR-5 (account lock) carries fraud and security risk. Non-compliance could result in loss of money transmitter license.",
    "stakeholders_affected": [
      { "group": "End Users (Wallet Holders)", "impact": "Directly affected by verification tiers, transfer limits, account locking, and notification policies. UX quality depends on clear communication of these rules." },
      { "group": "Compliance / Legal Team", "impact": "Owns KYC and sanctions requirements. Must audit that BR-1, BR-4 are enforced without exception. Liable for regulatory reporting." },
      { "group": "Customer Support", "impact": "Sole unlock authority for locked accounts (BR-5). Volume directly tied to lock frequency and user confusion about verification tiers." },
      { "group": "Finance / Treasury", "impact": "Settlement finality (BR-3) affects reconciliation. Daily limits affect cash flow and liquidity planning." },
      { "group": "Product Management", "impact": "Owns the policy thresholds (limits, notification amounts). Must balance growth with compliance." },
      { "group": "Banking / Payment Partners", "impact": "Rely on settlement finality and sanctions compliance. Partner agreements likely mandate these controls." }
    ]
  },

  "confidence": {
    "level": 72,
    "reasoning": "The spec is reasonably clear on core rules but has several gaps: (1) partially-verified withdrawal rights are ambiguous, (2) sanctions screening timing relative to settlement is unspecified, (3) locked account behavior for inbound transfers is unstated, (4) daily limit window definition (calendar day vs rolling 24h) is unspecified, (5) self-transfer policy is missing. The core rules are well-defined but these gaps prevent full confidence.",
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "KYC gating for send/withdraw (BR-1) — unambiguous",
      "Settlement irreversibility at T+1 (BR-3) — clear",
      "Sanctions blocking (BR-4) — clear",
      "Account lock after 5 consecutive failures (BR-5) — clear",
      "Notification threshold at >$1,000 (BR-6) — clear"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Whether partially-verified accounts can withdraw (spec only says unverified cannot)",
      "Daily limit window definition — calendar day or rolling 24 hours",
      "Sanctions screening at settlement time vs only at initiation",
      "Behavior of locked accounts for inbound transfers and balance visibility",
      "Whether a successful login after 4 failures resets the counter (implied but not stated)"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "Partially-verified accounts exist as a distinct tier between unverified and verified — the spec mentions them for limits but not for other capabilities",
      "Daily transfer limit resets on calendar day boundaries (midnight in account's timezone)",
      "A successful login resets the consecutive failed attempt counter",
      "The $1,000 notification threshold is exclusive (strictly greater than, not greater-than-or-equal)",
      "Account lock applies to authentication only, not to inbound fund receipt",
      "Pre-settlement cancellation is permitted since the spec only prohibits post-settlement reversal"
    ]
  },

  "escalations": [
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "The spec states unverified accounts cannot 'send or withdraw' and gives daily transfer limits for verified ($5,000) and partially-verified ($500) accounts, but does not specify whether partially-verified accounts can withdraw. Only unverified is explicitly barred from withdrawal.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["End Users", "Compliance", "Product Management"],
      "options": ["A: Partially-verified can withdraw up to their $500 daily limit", "B: Only fully verified accounts can withdraw (conservative reading)", "C: Partially-verified can withdraw with a separate, lower withdrawal limit"],
      "recommendation": "Option B — in regulated financial services, the conservative interpretation protects against compliance risk. Clarify with compliance team."
    },
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "The daily transfer limit window is not defined. Is it a calendar day (midnight reset) or a rolling 24-hour window from the first transfer? This significantly affects limit enforcement behavior.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["End Users", "Engineering", "Product Management"],
      "options": ["A: Calendar day reset at midnight (user's local timezone)", "B: Calendar day reset at midnight UTC", "C: Rolling 24-hour window from first outbound transfer"],
      "recommendation": "Option B — UTC midnight is simplest to implement consistently, avoids timezone manipulation, and is industry standard for financial limit windows."
    },
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "Sanctions screening timing: the spec says transfers 'must be blocked automatically' but does not specify whether screening occurs only at initiation or also at settlement. A recipient could be sanctioned between initiation and T+1 settlement.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Compliance", "Legal", "Banking Partners"],
      "options": ["A: Screen at initiation only", "B: Screen at both initiation and settlement", "C: Screen at initiation + continuous monitoring of pending transfers"],
      "recommendation": "Option C — this is the most compliant approach and aligns with OFAC guidance. At minimum, Option B. This needs legal review."
    }
  ],

  "rejection_reasons": []
}
